vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on avionics bay due to high heat dissipation
risk.mit: Implement thermal management system to ensure avionics bay operates within safe temperature range

risk.desc: Wing spar socket may experience excessive load during extreme maneuvers
risk.mit: Review load distribution and consider reinforcing wing spar socket design

risk.desc: Landing gear mount may not provide sufficient clearance for landing gear operation
risk.mit: Adjust landing gear mount design to ensure adequate clearance for landing gear movement

risk.desc: GPS mount may not be adequately protected from environmental factors
risk.mit: Add protective housing for GPS mount to shield from environmental elements

risk.desc: Service hatch may not provide sufficient access for maintenance
risk.mit: Increase size of service hatch to ensure easier access for maintenance tasks

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Nacelle loads routed through wing spar into fuselage spine
Heat from avionics dissipated through fuselage shell
Tail motion transmitted through ruddervator to v_tail
Service access for avionics bay maintenance
Electrical/data harnesses pass through to avionics bay

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: attach wing spar to fuselage spine first
route harnesses through avionics bay access

risk.desc: Potential corrosion due to maritime environment.
risk.mit: Use salt-fog resistant materials and coatings.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance for moving parts.

risk.desc: Inadequate thermal management for high heat flux.

risk.mit: Conduct load testing and reinforce critical structures.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to lack of alignment aids on tilt axis
risk.mit: Add alignment pins or guides to the tilt axis flange

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion causing misfit in press-fit interfaces
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion coefficients in assembly tolerances and allow for slight clearance

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling in motor and ESC pockets
risk.mit: Increase the size of cooling fins and ensure unobstructed airflow

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress through access covers
risk.mit: Use waterproof seals and ensure proper sealing of access covers

risk.desc: Risk of over-tightening during assembly leading to damage
risk.mit: Provide clear torque specifications and use calibrated tools

physics_paths.flow_path: Cooling air flow from nacelle inlet to motor and ESC pocket
physics_paths.thermal_path: Heat dissipation from motor and ESC to external air via fins
physics_paths.motion_path: Servo-driven worm gear rotation from tilt axis to nacelle

physics_paths.service_path: Access covers for motor and ESC replacement

risk.desc: The mass estimate of 2500g is conservative and may need adjustment based on actual material properties.
risk.mit: Conduct a detailed mass estimation using actual material densities and adjust the design accordingly.

risk.desc: The tilt axis may experience increased wear due to salt-fog exposure, potentially reducing the mechanism's lifespan.
risk.mit: Apply a corrosion-resistant coating to all metal components and ensure proper sealing of all interfaces.

risk.desc: The clearance arc may not provide sufficient clearance for the pushrod under extreme tilt angles.
risk.mit: Review the clearance arc dimensions and adjust as necessary to ensure full tilt range operation.

risk.desc: The access hatch may not be sufficiently sealed, allowing moisture ingress.
risk.mit: Implement a water-resistant seal around the access hatch to prevent moisture from entering the assembly.

risk.desc: The bearing retainer may not provide adequate support under high loads, potentially leading to bearing failure.
risk.mit: Review the bearing retainer design and ensure it can adequately support the bearing under all load conditions.
